verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a soluble protein lacking a transmembrane domain or any surface-anchoring mechanism. It localizes to the intra-acrosomal compartment of sperm (an intracellular vesicle), not to the plasma membrane outer face. Although it participates in sperm-egg fusion complexes, it does so as a soluble component recruited transiently to other membrane proteins' vicinity, not via stable surface exposure by its own mechanism. The protein body itself is not accessible from the extracellular face.
